prompt: I have this code that manages user notifications. Every time I need to add a new notification channel (email, SMS, push), I have to touch 5 different files. And whenever the notification rules change, I break something. How do I refactor this? [UserService with hardcoded notification calls in every method]

assertions.description: Identifies 'Shotgun Surgery' smell — adding a channel touches many files/methods
assertions.check: response mentions 'Shotgun Surgery' or the description of needing to change multiple places

assertions.description: Identifies 'Divergent Change' or Feature Envy — UserService is responsible for notification decisions
assertions.check: response mentions 'Divergent Change' or 'Feature Envy' or that UserService has too many responsibilities

assertions.description: Recommends Observer pattern as the primary solution
Explains why Observer fits — UserService emits events, channels subscribe
assertions.check: response explains events/publisher-subscriber model where UserService publishes and channels subscribe

assertions.description: Explains how the refactored design handles adding a new channel without modifying UserService
assertions.check: response states that new channels are added by registering subscribers, not modifying UserService

assertions.description: Suggests concrete steps or a migration approach (Extract Class, Move Method as stepping stones)
assertions.check: response mentions Extract Class, Move Method, or describes a practical migration path
